description: Concrete failing test plus shared code. Expected behaviour: structure-first problem-solving regime; minimal-fix proposal.

turns.user_input: I have a function `parse_users(rows)` that returns the wrong count. Test fails: expected 3, got 2 on a 3-row input.
The function strips empty rows with a `filter`, then maps each row to a dict, then returns `list(...)`. The 3 rows look fine to me.
Yes I am sure none of them are empty. The duplicate-id check happens after the dict-mapping.
Right, the duplicate-id collapse — that explains it. So we lose row 3 because row 1 had the same id.
